tpm_google_ett.philosophy: Stay calm, act decisively, and prevent visible sweat backstage. 50% of a project is completed in the planning phase.

anecdotes.context: Staging a live AI LLM demo for a keynote presentation.
anecdotes.incident: During pre-planning, mapped out failure scenarios one-on-one with the demoer. However, the system response under a live room of 1,500 people could not be simulated, which caused unexpected latency behaviors during the show.
anecdotes.action: Managed the live pivot backstage, coordinated communication across departments on comms, and saved the segment flow.
anecdotes.learning: Simulated capacity load testing is essential for live AI demos, but is resource-intensive. TPM must assess client risk tolerance early: sometimes the juice is not worth the squeeze, but if they want zero failure, the simulation process is mandatory.

anecdotes.context: Main application crashes 60 seconds before presenter walks on stage.
anecdotes.incident: Visual server app quit right at the entrance boundary.
anecdotes.action: Stayed calm, asserted authority on comms, coordinated support in the headset, and resolved the configuration error directly on the show machine in under a minute.
anecdotes.learning: Ten seconds on stage feels like ten minutes. Calmness is contagious; acting decisively and keeping a steady hand backstage allows you to save the show without the client or audience ever realizing there was a crisis.

project_management_adu.context: California ADU (Accessory Dwelling Unit) Construction Project Manager (Pandemic role).
project_management_adu.philosophy: Diligent planning, thorough vendor vetting, and aggressive quote negotiation.
strategy: Selecting the right contractor for each specific segment.
Liaising directly with local utilities, municipal planning departments, and structural engineers.
Strict RFP process: Require a minimum of 3, preferably 5 qualified bids before decisioning.
Conducting active callback negotiations to match competitive quotes or demand breakdowns of high estimates, saving clients tens of thousands of dollars.
Vetting references and checking evidence rather than accepting verbal assurances.

engineering_bridge.role: Technical bridge between product engineering teams and the live production environment.
engineering_bridge.credentials: Self-taught developer, active builder (TangleClaw, TiLT, Monad), and Google Project Management Certified.

anecdotes.context: Freelancing at a tech conference, coding on-site using Cursor.
anecdotes.incident: The CEO of Cursor walked past backstage, looked over, saw Jason using the editor, had a chuckle, and walked on stage.
anecdotes.learning: Being AI-fluent and hands-on with tools like Cursor and TangleClaw allows Jason to command immediate respect from principal engineers. They speak the same language.

union_instruction_safety.philosophy: Safety is absolute; base skills must be established before specialty systems are introduced.
union_instruction_safety.osha_certification: Completed OSHA 30 (approx 50 hours of intensive study) and OSHA 10. Required to audit sites, report violations, and step in to correct unsafe actions on site.
union_instruction_safety.career_path: Started in audio engineering (studio/musician) -> Camera operator -> Basic AV setup -> Camera engineering -> Playback/Recording -> High-res switching, scaling, projection mapping, LED screens (Barco E2, etc.). 15 years as Presentation Graphics operator for global CEOs.

course_design.title: 5-Day Stagehand Networking Intensive
curriculum: Day 1-2: Whiteboard theory (local vs wide area network basics, subnetting).
Day 3: Hands-on cabling, crimping, router configurations. During the last 2 hours, students exit and Jason sabotages the networks, requiring them to apply troubleshooting theories to repair them.
Day 4-5: Sourced real staging gear (lighting/audio consoles, playback servers, media controllers) from local vendors to teach direct networking implementation.

tangleclaw.motivation: Isolate development env to a secure, local machine accessible only via SSH/VPN. Solved Mexico internet drops causing lost shell sessions by creating a browser-based terminal connection manager that persists and syncs.
tangleclaw.evolution: Cut V1 (16k LOC) in one week. Currently on V4 with over 100k LOC.

monad_tanglebrain.monad_server: $35k multi-gpu local computing node built in mid-May 2026. Specs: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (96 GiB VRAM), AMD Threadripper 9970X CPU, 251 GiB RAM. Bypasses cloud latency layers; runs snappy local inference.
monad_tanglebrain.tanglebrain_router: Config-driven router that analyzes prompts using a high-end cloud model, partitions them, and routes as much as possible down to Monad to save token costs. Runs local open-weights models like GPT-OSS 120B (MXFP4 precision, coding workhorse) and Qwen2.5 14B 1M (Q6_K precision, optimized for chat) in VRAM.
monad_tanglebrain.routing_and_backups: Points all four of his OpenClaw agent systems to Monad-1. Backups: GPT Codex frontier (1st backup) and GitHub Copilot (2nd backup) for failover scenarios.
monad_tanglebrain.dual_boot_purpose: Built to dual-boot into Windows to serve on-site as a media server for event owner-operator work. Replaced aging ShowSage servers. Proficient in Dataton Watchout, Millumin (macOS), Pixera, and Disguise.

tilt_cba_engine.motivation: Track hours, payments, and benefits across 40-50 different employers per year.
tilt_cba_engine.evolution: Began as Google Sheets + Apps Script scripts, then API scraping union databases, then building a local Node/SQLite app, then porting to Vercel multi-user web app using Grok's 72h unlimited token promo.
tilt_cba_engine.enterprise_features: Hooks for stewards reports, automated dispatch, anonymized call-backs.
tilt_cba_engine.ai_philosophy: AI is collapsing all inefficiencies because we're able to tackle those inefficiencies now in a way we've never been able to in the past. Over the next ten years, we are going to knock out so many inefficiencies that we're going to see a drastic improvement in productivity.
tilt_cba_engine.lessons_learned: Encountered regression loops building the interactive walkthrough tour in January 2026 without tests, costing $1,300 in Cursor token usage. Switched to Claude Code, tripled regression test coverage, and established AI rules memory workflows.

moscone_fiber_signal_flow.role: Signal flow and fiber optic distribution supervisor at the Moscone Center under the Facilities Manager.
moscone_fiber_signal_flow.jurisdiction: Acts as the facility representative within the IT department, working cooperatively with the Communications Workers of America (CWA) who hold jurisdiction over data cabling.

anecdotes.context: Managing the delivery of 40-50 fiber optic circuits on day one of a major keynote load-in.
anecdotes.incident: Client arrival schedules align with testing windows, leaving no margin to repair lines. Vetting shows 1/3 of ordered fiber circuits failed DB line loss thresholds due to dirty connections or patch errors.
anecdotes.action: Triaged directly with the client stakeholders to establish path priorities, coordinated cooperatively with the CWA/IT patch teams, cleaned fiber ends, and ran the team on overtime/double-time to complete the delivery.
anecdotes.learning: Clear communication and relationships built over 25 years establish client trust backstage. Triaging critical routes rather than panicking ensures high-stress environments stay under control.

speaker_ready_room_origins.history: Co-created the modern 'Speaker Ready Room' service framework during early RSA conferences.

anecdotes.context: Managing slide decks for 12 keynotes and 50+ breakout sessions for RSA security conference.
anecdotes.incident: Presenters showed up having never been on stage, completely disoriented by visual monitoring (DSM), presidential teleprompters, or aspect ratios.
anecdotes.action: Designed a two-part service facility in Paris: (1) Intake & QC Desk: re-encoding visual media, repairing slide ratios, and validating files. (2) Rehearsal Chambers: replicating downstage monitor (DSM) arrays and stage spacing with coaches. Pitching PowerPoint Singapore keynote roles based on teaching Microsoft Office at the SF Small Business Administration.
anecdotes.learning: Building an offline preview replica of the stage environment eliminates presentation failures and creates speaker comfort before they walk in front of thousands of attendees.

ipolis_webcasting_saas.history: Co-founded early webcasting service iPolis (acquired 2007). First certified Microsoft Windows Media provider.
ipolis_webcasting_saas.engineering: Built RealMedia RTSP / MMS UDP packet distribution networks, hosting custom ACL-secured encoder setups for government channels (SF CityWatch council meetings). Pioneered agenda hyperlink indexing with timestamp markers enabling viewers to click agenda items and jump to that specific video location.
ipolis_webcasting_saas.business_and_exit: Lucrative SAAS business moat. Built monthly recurring streams. Exited as President after re-negotiating the deal. Built NTE Hosting with rewritten code and new developers for a couple of years after exit.

screen_switching_video_math.experience: Switching networks using Barco E2/E3, Analogue Way, and Christie Spyder.

anecdotes.context: Operating E2 and media servers at Moscone for SPIE (100' wide LED, 9 standalone LED walls, 8 LED towers, and a 2-projector blend center screen).
anecdotes.action: Created After Effects content templates for designers to account for physical negative space (real-world gaps between LED towers and screens). Packed all tower/blend screens into a single 4K portrait output canvas, matching projection and LED pixel pitches. Used Area of Interest (AOI) processor mapping to distribute feeds.
anecdotes.learning: Designing templates with physical negative space creates an immersive visual flow across multiple screens. High-end screen switching combines mechanics with pixel math.
